heading: Employees’ Life Insurance Fund
label: 8714 Employees’ Life Insurance Fund
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large (less applicability to ), (d).5 U.S.C. 2094(c)5 U.S.C. 2099(less applicability to § 10), .Aug. 17, 1954, ch. 752, § 5(c)68 Stat. 739“(c) (less applicability to § 10)”, (b), .Aug. 11, 1955, ch. 794, § 1(a)69 Stat. 676, (less applicability to § 10), .Apr. 11, 1958Pub. L. 85–377, § 172 Stat. 87In subsection (a), the words “of the Employees’ Life Insurance Fund” are substituted for “of a fund which is hereby created”. The proviso which made appropriations available to the Commission for salaries and expenses for the fiscal year 1955 available on a reimbursable basis for necessary administrative expenses for carrying out the purposes of this chapter is omitted as executed.Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

children.text: premium payments under an insurance policy purchased under this chapter; and
expenses incurred by the Office of Personnel Management in the administration of this chapter within the limitations that may be specified annually by appropriation acts.

children.label: b
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury may invest and reinvest any of the money in the Fund in interest-bearing obligations of the United States, and may sell these obligations for the purposes of the Fund. The interest on and the proceeds from the sale of these obligations, and the income derived from dividend or premium rate adjustments from insurers, become a part of the Fund.

No tax, fee, or other monetary payment may be imposed or collected by any State, the District of Columbia, or the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, or by any political subdivision or other governmental authority thereof, on, or with respect to, any premium paid under an insurance policy purchased under this chapter.

Paragraph (1) of this subsection shall not be construed to exempt any company issuing a policy of insurance under this chapter from the imposition, payment, or collection of a tax, fee, or other monetary payment on the net income or profit accruing to or realized by that company from business conducted under this chapter, if that tax, fee, or payment is applicable to a broad range of business activity.
